content: Colorectal cancer (CRC) is the third most common cancer worldwide. Molecular pathways: chromosomal instability (CIN, 65-70%, aneuploidy, APC/KRAS/TP53 mutations); microsatellite instability (MSI, 15%, defective mismatch repair, hypermutated); CpG island methylator phenotype (CIMP, 20-30%, epigenetic silencing). Lynch syndrome (hereditary non-polyposis colorectal cancer) caused by germline MMR gene mutations (MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, PMS2); lifetime CRC risk 10-80% depending on gene; also increased endometrial, ovarian, gastric, other cancers. Sporadic MSI usually from MLH1 promoter methylation. Familial adenomatous polyposis (FAP, APC mutation) causes hundreds-thousands of polyps, near-100% CRC risk without colectomy. RAS mutations (KRAS/NRAS, ~40%) predict resistance to EGFR inhibitors. BRAF V600E mutation (10-15%) associated with poor prognosis, right-sided tumors, MSI-H. HER2 amplification (3-5%) emerging target. Consensus molecular subtypes (CMS1-4) classify by gene expression patterns.
